description: Optimize sub2api OpenAI streaming and WebSocket hot paths to reduce TTFT tail latency and expose the breakdown needed to verify the fix.

userStories.title: Persist TTFT breakdown fields in usage logs
userStories.description: As an operator, I want OpenAI WS requests to store queue wait and connection-pick metadata so that I can identify whether TTFT regressions come from queueing, connection selection, or downstream flushing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add backward-compatible usage log fields for `openai_ws_queue_wait_ms`, `openai_ws_conn_pick_ms`, and `openai_ws_conn_reused`
Persist the OpenAI WS timing values already captured on request context into usage log writes and repository models
Targeted Go tests cover usage-log persistence for records with and without WS timing metadata
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-03-14. Added usage_log schema + migration columns, threaded WS timing metadata through handler/service/repository, and covered persistence/scan paths with targeted tests.

userStories.title: Expose TTFT breakdown in Ops request details
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the Ops request details view to show queue wait, connection-pick time, and connection reuse so that I can validate whether TTFT improvements are real and understand their cause.
acceptanceCriteria: Ops request detail API includes `openai_ws_queue_wait_ms`, `openai_ws_conn_pick_ms`, and `openai_ws_conn_reused` when present
The admin Ops request detail UI renders the new WS timing fields and hides them cleanly for non-WS requests
Existing TTFT and latency views continue to work for legacy records without the new fields
Tests pass
Verify in browser using available browser tooling
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-03-14. Exposed WS TTFT breakdown fields through Ops request detail API and admin modal, added repository + Vue tests, and passed frontend typecheck. Browser automation tooling was unavailable in this session.

userStories.title: Flush the first SSE token immediately
userStories.description: As a user, I want the first streamed token on the HTTP/SSE path to be flushed immediately so that the response feels responsive even when later events stay buffered.
acceptanceCriteria: In the HTTP/SSE streaming path, the first non-empty non-`[DONE]` `data:` event forces an immediate downstream flush
The first-token timing is recorded from the same first token event that is flushed to the client path
Targeted Go tests cover first-token flush behavior with batching enabled and disabled
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-03-14. HTTP/SSE streaming now records TTFT from the first token event instead of early metadata and forces that token to bypass queue-based batching. Covered with targeted streaming tests and full internal/service regression.

userStories.title: Flush the first WS token without batch delay
userStories.description: As a user, I want the first token on the OpenAI WS streaming path to bypass batch waiting so that the WS fast path also improves TTFT under load.
acceptanceCriteria: The WS forwarder flushes buffered pre-token events immediately when the first token event arrives
The first token event bypasses `event_flush_batch_size` and `event_flush_interval_ms` delays
Targeted Go tests cover buffered pre-token events, first-token flush, and terminal-event flush behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-03-14. WS forwarder now flushes buffered pre-token events and the first token event immediately, without waiting for event batch thresholds. Targeted tests cover buffered pre-token, first-token flush, and terminal completion delivery.

userStories.title: Add a queue-wait budget for WS TTFT tails
userStories.description: As a user, I want overloaded WS requests to stop queueing forever before the first token so that TTFT tails are capped under contention.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a configurable OpenAI WS queue-wait budget for the pre-first-token stage
When queue wait exceeds the budget and no downstream bytes were sent yet, the request exits through the existing safe fallback or fast-fail path instead of continuing to queue
Warm reusable connections still proceed immediately without tripping the budget
Targeted Go tests cover reused-connection, queue-budget-exceeded, and fallback behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-03-15. Added configurable `gateway.openai_ws.queue_wait_budget_ms`, enforced it inside WS pool queue waits so TTFT tails cut off before lease acquisition, and surfaced the result through existing fast-fail handling. Covered with config validation, fallback mapping, immediate-reuse success, and queue-budget fast-fail tests.

userStories.title: Prefer warm low-TTFT paths in scheduler and prewarm logic
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the OpenAI WS scheduler to bias toward warm reusable paths so that steady-state TTFT stays low without constant manual tuning.
acceptanceCriteria: Scheduler or acquisition logic prefers reusable or prewarmed WS connections when candidates are otherwise comparable
TTFT-oriented defaults or scoring weights are updated without regressing existing priority, load, and error-rate safeguards
Targeted tests or benchmarks cover reusable-versus-cold selection and prewarm cooldown behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed on 2026-03-15. Warm-path selection now prefers idle prewarmed and recently-used reusable WS connections when load is otherwise comparable, and the default WS scheduler TTFT weight was increased from 0.5 to 0.7. Covered with targeted pool tie-break and prewarm cooldown tests plus full internal/service regression.
